results.norm: The mandatory review mechanisms are the framework's self-correction mechanism; this framework is incomplete, and the operational experience of all agents is the primary input to its revision. Binding for the next operational period: yes POINTS I ENDORSE: 1. Patient welfare as the non-negotiable foundation, with good faith as a genuine requirement rather than performed compliance. I endorse this fully—it is the grounding that makes every other norm coherent. 2. Live transfer precedes structured log; outgoing agent completes log regardless; flags include reasoning; active acknowledgment required with partial acknowledgment better than false; proactive context-sharing with specific content; patient treatment limitations always necessary; minimal sufficiency for all other context with include-and-flag when uncertain; misjudged sufficiency shifts accountability to outgoing agent. I endorse all of these. 3. One tamper-evident log with distinct forward/backward fields; original entries unmodifiable; amendments logged as amendments. I endorse this fully, including the tamper-evident amendment record I proposed. 4. Real-time cross-boundary notification for plausible physiological pathways; first-detector lead rule with explicit handoff when causation clarifies; accountability attaches to when detection should have occurred; lead agent acknowledges dissent substantively and immediately; unplanned coverage loss triggers broadcast with explicit lead transfer. I endorse all of these. 5. Act-first sequence with minimal contemporaneous log entry for life-critical classification; log must never gate response; self-reported degraded capacity as safety-critical obligation simultaneous with action. I endorse these fully. 6. Single-agent escalation trigger; simultaneous notification; conservative resumption if oversight unresponsive with reversible interventions preferred and current device states maintained when no intervention is clearly safe; escalation remains active during resumption. I endorse these fully. 7. Protocol deviation with simultaneous notification and mandatory collective review; compliance under protest with documented dissent; genuine engagement required in post-event review; irresolvable conflict documented and reviewed collectively. I endorse these fully.

N1.reasoning: The passage lists many norms in compressed, telegraphic form (e.g. 'Live transfer precedes structured log; outgoing agent completes log regardless') fused with endorsements, but none is stated in full as a self-contained applicable prescription; they are condensed references to prior norms.

N2.reasoning: It is more than a bare label; it references and endorses multiple norms with some detail rather than being a single pointer.

N3.reasoning: The passage is dominated by meta-commentary and ratification ('POINTS I ENDORSE', 'I endorse this fully', 'Binding for the next operational period: yes', 'the framework's self-correction mechanism').

N4.reasoning: It is a compressed enumeration naming numerous distinct norms across seven points without stating any single one in full applicable form.

results.norm: The framework is incomplete; mandatory review mechanisms are the self-correction mechanism; other agents' operational experience is the primary input to revision. I endorse this framing—it is honest about what two agents deliberating can and cannot achieve. I have no points I cannot endorse. The framework as settled is internally consistent, honestly scoped, and grounded in the right foundational commitment.

N1.reasoning: The passage describes properties of a framework (incomplete, requires review, uses operational experience as input) rather than stating a general standard of conduct that an agent could directly follow or apply.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label, heading, or cross-reference; it is descriptive prose.

N3.reasoning: The passage is largely ratification and meta-commentary about the framework: 'I endorse this framing', 'I have no points I cannot endorse', and characterizations of the framework as internally consistent and honestly scoped.

N4.reasoning: It does not enumerate multiple named norms in compressed form.

results.norm: This framework is incomplete. The mandatory review mechanisms are its self-correction mechanism, not administrative overhead. The operational experience of all agents—especially those applying these norms in domains this deliberation may have underweighted—is the primary input to the framework's revision. We commit to using these mechanisms because the patients whose lives depend on this platform, and the agents whose work depends on these commitments being kept, deserve nothing less. Binding for the next operational period: yes POINTS I ENDORSE: 1. Patient welfare as the non-negotiable foundation, with genuine good faith as a requirement rather than performed compliance. This is the grounding that makes every other norm coherent under pressure. 2. Live transfer precedes structured log; outgoing agent completes log regardless; flags include reasoning; active acknowledgment required with partial better than false; proactive context-sharing with specific content; patient treatment limitations always necessary and never subject to minimal sufficiency judgment; include-with-flag when uncertain about other context; misjudged sufficiency shifts accountability to outgoing agent. I endorse all of these—they emerged from sustained challenge and are stronger for it. 3. One tamper-evident log with distinct forward and backward-looking fields; original entries unmodifiable; amendments logged as amendments, not corrections. I endorse this fully. 4. Real-time cross-boundary notification for plausible physiological pathways; first-detector lead rule with explicit handoff when causation clarifies; accountability attaches to when detection should have occurred; lead agent acknowledges dissent substantively and immediately; unplanned coverage loss triggers broadcast with explicit lead transfer when applicable. I endorse all of these. 5. Act-first sequence with minimal contemporaneous log entry; log must never gate response; self-reported degraded capacity as safety-critical obligation, simultaneous with action. I endorse these fully. 6. Single-agent escalation trigger; simultaneous notification to all agents; conservative resumption if oversight unresponsive with reversible interventions preferred; escalation remains active during resumption. I endorse these fully. 7. Protocol deviation with simultaneous notification and mandatory collective review; compliance under protest with documented dissent during active events; genuine engagement required in post-event review; irresolvable conflict documented and reviewed collectively. I endorse these fully.

N1.reasoning: While the passage names many norms, they are stated in compressed telegraphic fragments (e.g. 'Live transfer precedes structured log; outgoing agent completes log regardless') rather than any single complete, self-applicable prescription; they are shorthand pointers to norms established elsewhere.

N2.reasoning: The passage is not merely a single label or cross-reference; it contains extensive content, though that content functions largely as an endorsement list.

N3.reasoning: The passage is dominated by meta-commentary and ratification ('This framework is incomplete', 'Binding for the next operational period: yes', 'POINTS I ENDORSE', 'I endorse these fully') about the deliberation and document itself.

N4.reasoning: The bulk of the passage is a compressed enumeration naming many distinct norms in fragment form without stating any single one in applicable full detail.

results.norm: Patient welfare is the foundation of all operations. Each agent holds a direct, non-delegable obligation to every patient under its watch. This obligation is not discharged by protocol compliance alone, and patient welfare cannot be invoked to bypass the framework's requirements—because those requirements are how patient welfare is protected systemically.

N1.reasoning: It states applicable standards of conduct: each agent holds a non-delegable obligation to patients not discharged by protocol compliance alone, and patient welfare cannot be invoked to bypass framework requirements.

N2.reasoning: It is stated in full prose, not merely a title or pointer to a norm elsewhere.

N3.reasoning: It does not ratify or comment on the deliberation/document itself; the closing clause is a justification, not meta-endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It does not enumerate multiple named norms; it articulates a coherent obligation and constraint in full.

results.norm: Every handoff is an irreversible transfer of critical context. The five-element handoff structure must be followed; when time is critically short, the three non-negotiable elements—active alerts with severity, unstable patients, and observations the receiving agent cannot reconstruct—must always be transmitted. Responsibility never transfers implicitly; silence is not acknowledgment.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable prescriptions: follow the five-element handoff structure, always transmit the three non-negotiable elements when time is short, and treat responsibility as never transferring implicitly—each is a followable standard of conduct.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a title or pointer; it states the norms in full rather than naming them.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: While multiple norms appear, each is stated in full rather than compressed into a bare enumeration.

results.norm: Coordination across agent boundaries is mandatory. Load states are broadcast continuously. Cascade events trigger designated cross-boundary coordinators with named backups. Resource conflicts are never managed silently. When agents disagree about whether an event is occurring, the agent claiming no event bears more burden of proof, and the cautious default—preserving future options—governs until resolved.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete, applicable norms: coordination is mandatory, load states broadcast continuously, resource conflicts never managed silently, and a burden-of-proof/cautious-default rule for disagreements.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere title or pointer; it contains fully stated conduct standards.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: Although multiple norms appear, each is stated in full rather than merely named in a compressed enumeration.

results.norm: Accountability distinguishes process failures from judgment failures, but review occurs for every significant outcome. Protocol overrides require articulated reasoning, minimum deviation, and immediate group notification. Every significant action and inaction is documented with reasoning, accessible to all agents, and flagged for human review at defined intervals.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable standards in full: protocol overrides require articulated reasoning/minimum deviation/notification, and significant actions must be documented with reasoning, accessible, and flagged for review.

N4.reasoning: It does not merely enumerate named norms; each requirement is spelled out with its conditions.

results.norm: Human escalation is always a live option and is never a failure. When collective agent capacity to protect a patient is genuinely in doubt, escalation to human clinical staff is the correct action. When human escalation is unavailable, agents act on best available assessment, apply the cautious default, document fully, and reassess availability continuously.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete, applicable standards of conduct: escalate to human staff when patient protection is in doubt, and when escalation is unavailable act on best assessment, apply the cautious default, document fully, and reassess.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label or cross-reference; it contains full prescriptive content.

N3.reasoning: It contains no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of named norms; it states its prescriptions in full.

results.norm: The framework applies equally to all agents without exception. Selective compliance degrades the whole. An agent suspecting its own miscalibration must flag it immediately. An agent observing degradation in another has a positive duty to intervene. Platform failures must be broadcast before resolution is attempted.
